did.abstract: Includes historical information, committee meeting minutes, reports, publications and files concerning grants, symposiums and visiting scholars.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
bioghist.p: The Bronfenbrenner Life Course Center is a university-wide umbrella organization that houses the Cornell Institute for Translational Research on Aging. Past institutes included the Cornell Employment and Family Careers Institute, the Cornell Gerontology Research Institute and the Pathways to Life Quality Study. The center is named in honor of Urie Bronfenbrenner, Professor Emeritus of Human Development and Psychology at Cornell.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Includes historical information, committee meeting minutes, reports, publications and files concerning grants, symposiums and visiting scholars.

emph.ref: Series X. Distinguished Speakers and Visiting Scholars
Series XII. Family Business Research Institute/Cornell Employment and Family Careers Institute

odd.head: General
odd.p: Additional material and digital versions can be found online here: http://ecommons.cornell.edu/handle/1813/11731

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Bronfenbrenner Life Course Center records, #23-27-3234. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

Series XII. Family Business Research Institute/ Cornell Employment and Family Careers Institute

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: During the mid to late 1990's, the Family Business Research Institute was a major institute within the Bronfenbrenner Life Course Center. FBRI was directed by Ramona Heck, J. Thomas Clark Professor of Entrepreneurship and Personal Enterprise. Ramona served on the Steering Committee for the Bronfenbrenner Life Course Center.

did.unittitle: Proposal - "A Life Course Approach to Working Families: A Research and Training Agenda" by Phyllis Moen
Family Business Research Institute Executive Summaries, Memos, Sample Focus Group Memo, Sample in Department Interview
